options.a: Lord Cornwallis felt that the District Collector's efficiency of revenue collection would enormously increase without the burden of other work.
options.b: Lord Cornwallis felt that judicial power should compulsorily be in the hands of Europeans while Indians could be given the job of revenue collection in the district.
options.c: Lord Cornwallis was alarmed at the extent of power concentrated in the District Collector and felt that such absolute power was undesirable in one person.
options.d: Judicial work demanded a deep knowledge of India and good training in law, and Lord Cornwallis felt that the District Collector should be only a revenue collector.
